# Spec — 2026-05-14

## Executive Summary

- **anthropics/claude-code** (v2.1.141): Major feature release — `terminalSequence` field for hooks (desktop notifications without controlling terminal); Rewind menu "Summarize up to here" for partial compaction; background agents via `/bg` now preserve permission mode; spinner warms to amber after 10s; fixed dozens of edge cases including background service race conditions, MCP OAuth refresh concurrency, and fullscreen mode regressions
- **Kilo-Org/kilocode** (~30 commits): Configurable auto-compaction threshold; roll-call command for batch-testing model connectivity; i18n locale bundles for 18 languages (JetBrains); cloud history repo-only filter; agent-manager local state exclusion on startup; gateway transient item-id stripping; Wasteland product documentation
- **cline/cline** (CLI v3.0.2): `--worktree` flag to auto-create/run in a new git worktree per task; legacy CLI source removal (SDK CLI is now primary); SDK telemetry for auth/provider onboarding and compaction lifecycle; session status labels in history view; tool/task execution guidance clarification in system prompt
- **aider-ai/aider**: No new commits in last 7 days
- **continuedev/continue**: No new commits in last 7 days
- **Key new pattern**: Partial/selective compaction — Claude Code v2.1.141 adds "Summarize up to here" in the rewind menu, allowing users to compress only earlier context while keeping recent turns intact. Our compaction is all-or-nothing (full conversation summarization)
- **Key new pattern**: Git worktree isolation per task — Cline ships `--worktree` flag that auto-creates a detached-HEAD worktree per task so each agent works on isolated file state. We have no equivalent task-level worktree management
- **Key new pattern**: Hook terminal output control — Claude Code adds `terminalSequence` field to hooks JSON so hooks can emit desktop notifications/bells without a controlling terminal. Our hooks have no structured terminal output mechanism
- **Gap persists (from yesterday)**: Hook `continueOnBlock` rejection feedback, MCP project directory env var, agent observability headers — these remain the highest priority unimplemented items from yesterday's research
- **New gap**: Configurable compaction threshold exposed to user config (Kilo shipped `feat: configure auto compaction threshold`); our `autoCompact` is a boolean toggle with no user-configurable threshold

## What to build (prioritized)

### 1. Hook Rejection Feedback Loop (continueOnBlock)
- **What**: Add `continueOnBlock` option to PostToolUse hooks — when a hook rejects tool execution, feed the rejection reason back to the model as an error message so it self-corrects instead of halting
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.139 shipped this and v2.1.140-141 continued fixing edge cases around it. Our hooks system (`src/hooks/index.ts`) fires events and returns `HookResult` but has no path to feed rejection back into the orchestrator. This is the most impactful guardrail pattern for production use
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139 `continueOnBlock`; persistent across v2.1.140-141 fixes
- **Category**: config
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 2. MCP Project Directory Environment Variable
- **What**: Pass `ALEXI_PROJECT_DIR` (resolved from `process.cwd()` or project root) to MCP stdio server child processes in `src/mcp/client.ts:connectStdio`
- **Why**: Claude Code shipped `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` in v2.1.139. Our `connectStdio` at line 133 builds env from `process.env + config.env` but has no project directory. MCP servers that resolve relative paths (file tools, git tools) must hardcode paths in config today. Single line of code, high value
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139; Kilo-Org/kilocode already has this pattern in its MCP implementation
- **Category**: mcp
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 3. Configurable Auto-Compaction Threshold
- **What**: Expose the compaction trigger threshold (currently hardcoded at 80% of `maxContextTokens` in `src/compaction/index.ts:DEFAULT_WARNING_THRESHOLD`) as a user-configurable setting via `kilo.json` or project config
- **Why**: Kilo just shipped `feat: configure auto compaction threshold` this week. Our compaction system has a fixed 0.8 threshold and a boolean `autoCompact` toggle. Different models/tasks benefit from different thresholds — aggressive (0.6) for long-running tasks, permissive (0.9) for short tasks
- **Source**: Kilo-Org/kilocode PR #10218 "Configure auto compaction threshold"
- **Category**: config
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 4. Selective/Partial Compaction ("Summarize up to here")
- **What**: Add ability to compact only messages before a specified index while preserving recent turns verbatim. Extend `CompactionOptions` with an `upToIndex` parameter and add a `/compact-before` command
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.141 ships "Summarize up to here" in the rewind menu. Our compaction is all-or-nothing — `preserveRecent` keeps N recent messages but there's no way to selectively compress a middle range. This enables the user to retain recent debugging context while compressing setup/exploration history
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.141 — "Rewind menu: added 'Summarize up to here'"
- **Category**: core
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 5. Agent Observability Headers (OTEL Tracing)
- **What**: Inject `x-alexi-agent-id` and `x-alexi-parent-agent-id` headers on LLM API requests from subagents. Extend `src/providers/sessionHeaders.ts` to accept agent identity alongside session affinity
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.139 traces all subagent API calls with hierarchy headers and OTEL span attributes. Our session headers (`sessionHeaders.ts`) track session affinity but not agent identity. Multi-agent debugging requires knowing which agent originated each API call. This is 3 lines of code change
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139 — `x-claude-code-agent-id` / `x-claude-code-parent-agent-id` headers
- **Category**: provider
- **Size**: small (< 4h)
